mel.gl: sprinkle pure water on s.o. for purification
bundle of rattan carried as a load
rapprocher le bois du centre du foyer
cut the bark off a tree
de petites depenses pour une ceremonie
heat up roti on the fire
to wear by draping (scarf or turban)
a *shrub, remedy against stomach trouble, probably a member of the Melastomaceae family
probably a member of the Melastomaceae family
to store, to put in the right place
to be *able, to be *healthy, to be *cure|d
to *wear (wrapped around the neck or head)
a tree in the pea family
grande jarre ronde en terre, à chang
to set to cook (for the dead)
to *go too far, to go beyond the goal, to *cross, to by*pass
to *escape, to come out of binding; to loose urine or stool
come out of binding; to loose urine or stool
to *cover or fill with earth, to *bury lightly
to be *finished (of a long process, esp. defecating)
*evil-eye from s.o. who watches you eating
to *fall (dead leaves or branches), to cause to fall, to *shake down
a small flat *fish whose mouth is also flat
to *scorch, to seal or clean with fire
to have a *miscarriage, still-*birth, to be still born
to *scrape with a knife: to *shave a killed animal, to *scrape vegetables
petit sac en poil de chèvre
to *stretch, to hang up for display
to *hurt teeth, to set teeth on edge
to *saw or *cut lengthwise, to rip, to *split
to *turn around, to turn over, to *become
to have one's clothes *caught (on a thorn, a piece of wood ...)
to *stir up against, to call up ghosts against
to *pinch (of a person, a dog), to *bite lightly
the wound in a tree where the parasitic plant /¹nai/ has attached itself
to *gurgle (liquid in a moving container), to *slosh (of liquid)
leather *strap linking the beam of the plough with the yoke
manner of stamping or *striking with the hands alternately
to *peck (as a bird), to *bite (of a snake)
to *participate in a common fund, to unite for some project
to chatter, to babble, to prattle
instrument à rassembler la terre en tas
<*tooth> the teeth of the *comb of a loom
to *rub, to *grate (esp. radishes), to *scrape (one's back against, animals)
to *swirl, to *roll self up, to *curl up
to *cork, to *stop up, to *block up, to *fill up
to be too *thin, to lack thickness, to be *sparse
to *light (series of fires, children for amusement)
to *repeat after the leader in singing <to *sing>
a wild grass used to make *yeast for beer
a *pinch of, a *handful of, a *little (of a solid)
mesurer une longueur ou un volume
to *bring a log back towards the center of the *fire
way of tearing by pulling apart <*tear>
small *bag for flour or maize, purse
to sprinkle pure water on s.o. for purification
sprinkle pure water on s.o. for purification
to *deliver, to take s.o or sthg someplace, to *accompany
to be *upset, to be *irritated, to be *depressed
to *heap soil at the foot of a plant
to get out of a difficult situation, to *save one's life <*avoid>
get out of a difficult situation
to *throw, to propel along the length of something, to crawl
propel along the length of something
to be *ungrateful (towards parents or guru), quit one's guru
to insert, to instill, to bring in
to run, to flee, to jump
to explain the road to the deceased during the *funeral ceremony
to *eat, to stuff oneself with food
to *meet, to go *visit s.o., to *find, to *catch, to catch up with, to *reach
to season with spices fried in butter
season with spices fried in butter
to heat up roti on the fire
heat up roti on the fire
to *pull up (clothes), roll up at the waist
to *borrow an object that must be returned (not replaced)
leaves and branches of the small *bamboo ($niṅgalo)
*once upon a time, in the old days, in remote *time|s
to let fall slowly in the wind off the winnowing basket <*winnow>
to ripen artificially in hot bed, to dry-ferment
to *crunch, to *crackle, to be overripe and dried up <*dry>
to stoke, to stir up fire
large flatish *basket used for carrying manure
to encourage, to urge, to tempt
without eating, on an empty stomach <*fast *hungry>
*skewer (to pick fried bread, nep $bābar, out of hot oil)
to be *addicted to, develop a taste for
to *winnow (with an up and down motion)
to *pluck (an ear of grain) by pulling on it
*meat of a chicken strangled for a ritual
to be a cry-baby or to throw *tantrum|s
to return on the same day from a journey
return on the same day from a journey
*food chewed by a parent for a baby
pubic *hair; also used as an insult
to *remove by causing to slide out or away: to blow one's nose, to *clean entrails, to remove nit from hair
grande marmite en cuivre pr cuire le thé
qui a un bec de lièvre (homme)
qui a un bec de lièvre
to *squeeze, to *compress, to enter the flesh (as a stone in a shoe), to *squash

to cut or carve with a sawing motion; to *cut the throat (of birds); (rarely) to *saw wood
*strap or rope used to hang a lama's drum
1 seul est (= être le même, être d'accord)
to *shake the earth off earth clods
to be caught and *snag on sthg (clothes) <to *tear>
to *gather in a crowd, to be *crowded
to *burst open (cloth at the seam, pod in the sun, wood in fire..)
to *unstitch, to become *unsewn; to *undo, to *take down (a shelter, a roof)
*husk of maize, *shell of peanut, empty shell
to sit, to stay, to live, to remain
to *reach, to be *able to reach (stretching the hand or arm), to *suffice
a large bamboo *basket used for grain storage
to be *ready (of a man-made object); to be *grown up beyond early childhood (of a child)
to give s.o. to be married
to *heap up a measure, to be heaped
name of a neighbourhood in Pheta village
*integument (hard skin-like element which envelops muscle flesh)
to scoop out, to dip into (itr)
to *burn flour in memory of the dead
a few days ago, the other day, day-before-*yesterday
to *lower, to *gather towards oneself (e.g. with the sickle before cutting)
a large tight-weave carrying *basket, used for manure etc.
to sow (a lot at a time)
to *suffocate (while drinking or by inhaling dust)
to start a piece of basketware <*weave>
to *fall (as when tripped, not from a height)
hooked iron *rod used for guiding the thread when preparing the loom
*rice (in the field, or collected but unhusked)
to have *accomplished a section of work
to be stricken by a ghost
having long thick hair (of a goat, a dog, etc.), *hairy
a *disease of rice or sojabean (growing straight without developped grain)
(*imperative of verb roots ending in a short vowel)
to be weakened by an acid contact (teeth) <*tooth>
to *come off of the *handle, to come out (of a handle)
*beer (a thick fermented drink made from grain)
to *stir up and down with a ladle
plate of *offerings, constituting a symbolic representation of the universe
constituting a symbolic representation of the universe
*presents distributed when assigning work for some ceremony (e.g. /³kewa/), by ext. the meeting where this is done
by ext. the meeting where this is done
to *bend towards the ground (branches, old person)
to *split (wood), to break (a nut), to split rocs to make building stones
split rocs to make building stones
personal *name of an ancestor of Tamangs
to be difficult to swallow, to *choke
a bamboo *mat used for drying grains
*please (polite insistance with the imperative); indication of urgency
dieu sur le toit (à cornes ou à fleurs)
what do you want?, so what?, of course! <*exclamation>
to get *entangled, to roll up a tangled thread without disentangling it
roll up a tangled thread without disentangling it
to *lose stool or urine in small amount
reste de la pâte après filtrage (de la bière)
reste de la pâte après filtrage
a tree of the laurel family
throwing earth on both *side|s of a dig (plow, pig rooting)
of greasy appearance (of blister from burn or insect bite)
to *strip with knife, to *peel (raw potatoes), to *bark (wood), to cut the surfaceoff with hoe (as when cleaning field-walls)
to *wear a shawl, to wrap (the end of a sari over the shoulder)
to *even out (a piece of wood, a bundle of sticks)
to clear a field (for first cultivation)
*pivot serving as the *hinge of a door
to *fail to recognize, to *tease by giving the wrong thing
the assembly of *god|s or Buddhas (not the historical Buddha alone)
an earthen *basin used for mixing beer, washing etc.
to put, to pour, to serve
to accumulate property by saving, to establish a household
paillasse de pierre et boue dans la cour
mari de soeur cadette du père
eye-*bolt holding the *chain of the door
explode (to_, make the noise of a gun)
to *break off (tr. e.g. maize), to *prize off (with fingers, plaster etc)
to *warm up an empty cooking pot before pouring the food to be cooked in it (bread or pop-corn)
gde bassine à cuire en cuivre rouge
to have an *appetite, to be easy to please (about food)
*greeting| done by touching one's forehead to someone's feet <*prostration >
the inside *pot of the distillation contraption
to peel (to strip skin with hands)
to make an *offering, to *give (highly honorific of recipient)
to tidy up a dry field after sowing
tidy up a dry field after sowing
*skull of a dead man (used for religious purposes)
an *exclamation to chase animals away from cultivated fields
femme du frère de la mère
to shout, to scream, to howl
handful of millet meal ($ḍhim̐ḍo) formed into a *ball in the hand
to *avoid (s.o., the enemy, a dangerous animal)
to *step on one's own clothes, to *fasten to, to moor
to be separated, esp. *remote in a lineage
*excited, demanding (child); to be impatient, to start an action without due preparation (adult)
start an action without due preparation
being *missing just at the wrong time
mari de la soeur de l'épouse
to *lop off, to cut as with a scythe
to be *invalid, to be terminally ill
exorciser (en jetant une statue de farine)
to be *cold and damp, to get cold, to cool off
to strike, to thresh, to pound (grain)
a very hard *stone used to pound in the handle of a quern (hand-mill)
*upstream, up, towards the inner part of the house
towards the inner part of the house
the sound of the fluffing bow <*fluff>
cooked *flour that has stuck to the bottom of the cooking-pot
to *distort face (before crying), to bring in (belly)
to *drizzle; to be about to rain
seating place of the lamas during a funeral ceremony
to *find, to *get one's share, to be *sufficient
to *spread (of disease), to be *contagious, to *stain; to be *sticky
a plant, a broad-leaved relative of /²saŋan/
to *smooth off the eyes of bamboo
to *bang clothes on the ground by little strokes
a large copper or earthen *pot for storing grain or water
to *squeeze out (juice from fruit, water from laundry)
pierres blanches marquant le milieu du champ
to *itch in the throat, to taste *pungent
*slough (of a snake), hard upper-*skin on chicken feet, integument
to *fit through, be able to go through
to *come out of the ground (of water in the monsoon, not at a spring)
to *shake a tree after climbing in it
to *cut with a heavy blow, to turn a field over by hand
turn a field over by hand
to have an *astringent taste (esp. Sichuan pepper)
mari de la soeur aînée de la mère
to *smother, to keep covered (rice etc) to finish cooking
to *follow everywhere, to whine and beg to go along (of children)
whine and beg to go along
to *trim (hair), to cut carefully straight (stick), to *prune
to *splutter, to blunder, to be mixed up, to be *diverted (from work)
a parasitic plant which yields a yellow sweet *berry
wooden *bar used for fastening the door
to boast, to be proud, vain
to *stir the cooking batter of maize or millet
boiled *grain: ideally *rice, but more frequently maize or millet; a *meal
to *think about, to come to mind
to be *full to the brim, to *overflow
femme du frère cadet du père
to *lean, to support oneself (e.g. with a cane on the ground), to press something on the ground
with a big *fluttering *noise, in a flock of birds
to break down the lumps in a wet field
break down the lumps in a wet field
person of *mixed descent, and clan issued thereof
an earthen cooking *pot for roasting grain
*cyst, *lump in flesh or fat (mostly of goats)
*ceremony held on the day of death

libation of food that will be eaten later
to *sprout vegetatively from roots, etc., not seed

of the size of, like, as *much as

pot de bois tourné, à col

hard *skin of the tongue of animals,

call for the bullocks to turn around at the end of a furrow

to *spend generously, to organize with munificence
the *fold of skin under the beak of a cock

a large *horn used in the Buddhist ritual

to get *mix|ed in evenly (mostly spices in food)
proper *name (both male and female, always given as first name)

top of the Nepalese traditional suit

a *whisk; any plant from whose forking branch a whisk can be made
to *turn up a field by hand, to *dig with a pick
any of a number of plants whose seed catches on clothes

to *fall (of fruit), to *flow by itself from the teat (of milk)

a plant of the mint family

give away a daughter in marriage

which is found on mustard greens
name of a neibourghood of Risiangku <*placename>
person of mixed origin, without a proper *caste

cooked without fat or eaten without sauce

mari de soeur aînée du père
to shave, to shear, to cut hair

the decease caused by such ghost in a living child
to *fashion (of cooked rice, a religious figurine)
proper *name for a woman born on Wednesday
to *wrap up; %fig.| to put aside, to *save

wicker *trunk with a lid (made of bamboo strips)
to *fry in a little oil before adding water
adjective suffix, esp. on colour terms
